notes: Both disagreement entries name the doctrinal axis substantively. The 'mātrā-sparśāḥ' entry distinguishes Shankara's etymological derivation (mātrā = measuring/marking organ; mātrā-sparśāḥ = sense-organs in contact with objects) from Ramanuja's derivation (mātrās = subtle elements / tanmātras), and explains why the underlying ontology differs while both arrive at 'sense-contact' as the operative meaning. The 'titikṣasva' entry distinguishes Shankara's reading (don't be moved positively or negatively) from Ramanuja's reading (duty-enabling endurance) and explains the practical convergence: stability not coupled to sensation polarity. Both entries pass.
